name: Heat Stress (Hyperthermia)
description: Occurs when cattle cannot dissipate body heat in high ambient temperature and humidity. High-producing dairy cows most susceptible. Can cause severe production and reproductive losses.

diagnostic_criteria.definitive: Temperature >39.5°C combined with hot weather conditions and clinical signs

treatment_info.veterinary_treatment: Cooling therapy, IV fluids in severe cases. Electrolyte supplementation.
supportive_care: Provide shade and cool water immediately
Spray cool water on body (especially neck and flank)
Feed during cooler hours (early morning / evening)
treatment_info.first_aid: Move to shade. Spray with cool (not ice cold) water. Offer fresh cool water. Reduce handling.
treatment_info.do_not: Do not use ice-cold water directly – can cause shock. Do not move animals unnecessarily.
